# Information Hierarchy Contract

Purpose: stage information so nothing important is lost and nothing overwhelms the first screen.

Required fields:
- Primary content: visible first.
- Secondary content: one click/tap away.
- Tertiary content: drawer, modal, details route, or expandable section.
- Hidden/admin content: visible only when relevant.
- Navigation plan.
- Disclosure pattern.
- Overload risks.
- What leaves the first screen.

Examples:
- Wine list: list and filters are primary; help-me-choose is secondary; cellar notes are tertiary/admin.
- Manager brief: today/status/action is primary; setup/history/editing is secondary.
- Event intake: public inquiry is primary; internal tracker is secondary/admin.
- Analytical model: recommendation/confidence is primary; formula receipts are secondary/tertiary.

Pass:
```text
The user sees the job first, then can reach deeper tools intentionally.
```

Fail:
```text
The first route shows every workflow, every detail table, and every helper at once.
```
